description:
Advances: specled.verification

Deliverable:
Full detail: refined plan Stage 1 (mini-plan think-t2d run 20260702-071642) + ADR specled.decision.evidence_based_attribution (authored at distill).
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex run_command/3 (~1862-1909): rewrite the /bin/sh wrapper with POSIX job control so the target runs in its own process group and timeout kills the group. Script shape: cd <root> || exit 127; set -m; (<target>) > <tmp_out> 2>&1 &; echo $! > <tmp_out>.pgid; wait $!. POSIX set -m guarantees background jobs get their own pgroup (pgid = job-leader pid) on macOS bash-as-sh and Linux dash — zero new deps, no setsid binary (absent on stock macOS), no erlexec.
- On timeout: read <tmp_out>.pgid, System.cmd("kill", ["-KILL", "--", "-<pgid>"]), then Port.close(port). SIGKILL directly, no TERM grace (S2's artifact protocol is line-flushed). Tolerate missing/empty pgid file (timeout raced the script's first line) by falling back to today's Port.close-only behavior.
- wait $! propagates the target's own exit status — preserves specled.verify.command_exit_code_recorded exactly.
- after-block cleanup additionally removes <tmp_out>.pgid.
- merged_tagged_tests_results funnels through the same run_command, so the merged run gets the kill for free (hard prerequisite for S2's artifact integrity).
- NO spec requirement text change: the stable must-requirement specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced already mandates the kill — this is code catching up to spec. ADD scenario specled.verify.scenario.command_timeout_kills_process_group (append-only safe) covering that requirement.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check (never hand-edit).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: extend the existing timeout test in test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs (~948, 'command verification times out on slow commands') or add a sibling, tagged @tag spec: "specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced". Target writes 'echo $$ > <pidfile>; sleep 30'; after verify returns, poll kill -0 <pid> via System.cmd and assert the process is gone.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenario specled.verify.scenario.command_timeout_kills_process_group.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests/**
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- test/integration/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- No requirement statement changes — scenario addition only.
- No :timeout-fact changes (s65.7 landed that; leave its shape alone).
- No env-var plumbing, no artifact, no attribution, no formatter (S2).
- No change to which exit code a timeout records.
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer; @impl with explicit behaviour module; escape interpolation in doc strings.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S1: Process-group kill on verification command timeout
